question_text: What happens to gas particles when the temperature of a gas increases?

options.label: A
options.text: They move more slowly and closer together

options.label: B
options.text: They move faster and spread out more

options.label: D
options.text: They arrange themselves in a fixed pattern

explanation: Temperature is a measure of how fast particles are moving. When temperature increases, gas particles gain energy and move faster, spreading out and taking up more space. This is why heated gas expands.
